theory.canonical_statement: ID 484 formalizes the balance between unitary evolution and non unitary dissipation. For the Trawin Zero Point to maintain stability, the jump operators must be normalized against the coupling constant. This prevents the system from overheating during the phase transition to matter. Dissipation Control: Regulates the rate at which Landauer heat is cleared from the registers. Unitary Fidelity: Protects the coherent Stillness of the core during external grid synchronization.
